intro: Chamaemelum nobile, commonly known as chamomile or English chamomile, is a low-growing, flowering plant in the daisy family. This plant has been used for centuries to promote relaxation, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve sleep quality due to its soothing and calming effects. The bioactive constituents of Chamaemelum nobile include flavonoids, terpenoids, and apigenin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to treat mild insomnia, digestive issues, and skin irritations.
